item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure which is more depressing: the possibility that we didn't do well in the rankings - 4-digit number means between 999 and 9998 universes did better than us - or the possibility that, despite the fact that we passed the message ~50000 times, we still performed well, if not exceptionally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was so awesome to read! The twist ending was great and added a touch of levity after the wild ride (I assume our universe didn't fare well in the rankings). I'm gonna send this to a friend that also browses the site but with no context >:)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
running a copy of Windows Vista OS

This part made me scream in terror.

But seriously, this was really good. I didnt see the ending coming at all. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"oh look, it's the 'forward this email or you die' SCP. Boring"

…
…
…

Magnificent execution, the end makes this a firm upvote. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God damn you, Dr. Winter!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent, especially the payoff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved the twist ending! It was a pretty good "cursed article" story up until that point, but the mind screw conclusion makes this an enthusiastic +1 from me. Thanks for writing it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that was a mind fuck. Been a while since I was so unnerved by a scip. And here I thought I had finally built a tolerance. Have my +1.

item.title: r/SCPDeclassified
item.content:encoded: For those who don't quite understand, here is an r/SCPDeclassified link for you!

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent change by dysty was reverted, as it introduced substantial new content without author permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow I got got so hard. I don't think anything on this site got me this hard lately.
Passionate burning +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder if our universe would have scored better if the survey didn't fall into the hands of the organization obsessed with keeping things contained by any means necessary :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Doctor Huxtable? Wow, that makes The Cosby Show a lot freakier knowing he was in the Foundation all along.

This got me, though. I was waiting for a jumpscare image for fast scrollers, so I feel extra trolled. Good work!

item.title: Not an SCP
item.content:encoded: This ain't no SCP this is a damn story!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: VERY clever, though I think it would've been better to actually offer a yes or no choice in which the reader would get the ending or be left with the urge to pass it on even after the tab is closed. Though I'm not sure how one would put that into an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay now that I've read this skip several times and gone through the comments, I still love this skip, just for different reasons. The bait-and-switch annoyed me at first, but after reflecting upon the fact that that was clearly the joke, I like it even more. Take your +1, you bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm really glad to see this getting the attention it deserves. I loved it as a draft, and its even better now. Kudos!

I will confess, I'm really glad you didn't listen to me and change the ending. I've had time to think on it, and it would have lost something special without it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a neat idea and generally a fun read, but this was what kept me from upvoting. The tonal switch between trying to draw the reader in and the narration was jarring, it would be better to fully commit to the former.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for advice. It seems though like you had many other problems with this, so changing this specific wouldn't have changed your vote anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although that's where I downvoted, so YMMV.

It wasn't just that, the ending could be a lot less flippant and have the same content.

I really wanted to like this, but IMO the execution just isn't quite there.

item.title: Re: +111
item.content:encoded: Hey, you desesrve it! Normally, I'm not a fan of meta skips, but this was a tight, delightful read that is totally deserving of its score.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

But *sob* I accidentally ruined it for myself by clicking the wrong link first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Judging by the current score I don't think it's that much of a concern and I'd rather leave it in than risk some people missing the point of the final note. Thanks for the feedback though, appreciated.
This is my first scip ever to break the +100 barrier (in fact it's also the first one to break +50) and I have to say I didn't expect such a postive reception. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just realized, when this skip came out a lot of people were telling me "you need to read this SCP its so good". And then I went and told other people that… And they told more people.

This SCP subconsciously chain lettered itself. Which, while almost certainly unintentional, is just another level of meta to appreciate.

Well played.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Psh, count me as someone who would have forwarded the damn letter along. If everyone in the Foundation has 3 minutes to give to containing something, then it's contained.

Stupid test! :D

item.title: +100 in two days
item.content:encoded: People enjoy being creatively trolled. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that was the one part I was iffy about as well. I understand the reasoning behind it from an authorial perspective, and the italics do a decent job of keeping the 4th wall up. I'd say it should be cut, but I'm unsure as to how you would replace it and get the same effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it, it really drew me in and had a great twist.

My one comment, though. This bit:
"A moment of hesitation comes as you press the key,

What horrors await beyond the veil?

What lurks just outside the event horizon?

A single message flashes across the screen:"

I personally don't really think it's necessary to have it in there, I think that could just be deleted. It sort of sucked me out of it to have this second-person narrative happen in the middle of the document. I can assume from the context that the reader pressed "N", and assume that the stuff coming up after that is what shows up on the screen next, without that happening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ [Spoiler] When you finish reading it, you will scream...
- ...at the author for creatively trolling you. :P.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am sitting here alone in the house. There is nobody else in sight or within earshot.

Possibly the best possible time to read this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is my new favorite article of February. The prose itself is nothing special, but this is a concept that swings on originality and thematic execution alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This read like a backwards ass Hitch Hiker's Guide to the Galaxy and I fucking love it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hooooooo this gave me goosebumps. Personally I have a total weakness for meta SCP's, but this one by far sucked me in like a void. I legitimately almost impulsively sent the link to this article to one of my friends just to see how he'd react.

And then I read the end and snorted. Serious SCP's that can also make me laugh absolutely deserve a +1 in my books.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I saw the edit with my name on it I was completely spooked, you are able to make this article appear to be true! I loved it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to see this up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I put it there there to keep the footnotes from showing up near the top when you first open the article, but I'm glad it found another use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is exactly how an article should subvert our expectations. This is a neat piece and all its parts worked like clockwork.

Now, even though I am not still convinced about the last note was delivered, I actually fell for it and there were also too many good things just not to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Supporting this because I wasn't the only one. Can't believe it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ve been wanting to see a chain email SCP for a while, and this really delivered on that. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This screwed with me in ways you cannot possibly imagine. Well done.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's been a while since an SCP article both surprised me and me go "GODDAMMIT OP YOU SON OF A BITCH" at its surprise. I was nodding along, ready to give a lukewarm upvote at a well-done but tired "meta eldritch" premise…

…and then you got me. You fucking got me. Enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if this is intentional, but I absolutely enjoy all the blank space after the article is done. It's a great way of simulating the experience of just sitting there, shocked at the outcome, taking it all in. Hell, I actually did do that IRL. So, well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nothing hidden in all that blank space? I'm shocked! (I saw the other hidden stuff, though, don't worry.)

Chain mails as containment/sociological testing is brilliant. I love everything about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was hesitant about this at first, but after letting myself roll with the experience it was a really fun read. The note at the end was also a clever subversion of what I expected would happen (not sure what I expected actually, but it wasn't that).

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done! Glad to see this on the mainsite. It was quite an unusual and interesting read in the forums. +1 from me here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know… I thought something was fishy for a second at the top, but then I got so swept up in the article I stopped thinking about those possibilities. When I reached the end the first thing I thought was "oh good… wait I KNEW IT".

+1. Preferably more if I could though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this, and I wanted to hate it so much. But instead I let myself get drawn in (ha ha ha), and by the end of it my heart was pounding. I would have upvoted at that point, to be honest, just for the effect it gave me, but then you come down with the final note and bam. I like articles that set up a narrative just to pull it out from underneath you, and this is one of the best examples of that I've seen. Well friggin' done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a brilliant, brilliant take on the chain letter. That twist at the end… very nice.

Fantastic! This thing had so many twists and turns.

At first I felt the images were really hokey but they gradually started to cause more and more tension and anxiety. I didn't like where the article was going until I completed the first half and it struck me that I had read what was more or less a tale to give context.

The secondary article leads up to a conclusion I genuinely DID NOT see coming. At all. I was completely blind-sided.

Equal parts tense and amusing. This is really, really good imo.

item.title: Posted
item.content:encoded:
There it is, my first meta SCP. Big thanks to Zyn, Toa_Vine, Leveritas, Westrin and BlazingTrail who critted this as a draft. I hope you enjoy the read.

Edit: forgot to credit my awesome sister for making the images.
